whatThisIs: Compute Devine predicted body weight (PBW) from height and sex - 50 kg for men plus 2.3 kg per inch over 60 inches, 45.5 kg for women plus the same per-inch increment - and convert it into the ARDSNet lung-protective tidal-volume target at 4-8 mL/kg PBW (default 6 mL/kg, the trial's primary arm). Sophie Well returns the PBW in kilograms and the Vt target range in millilitres so a respiratory therapist or intensivist can verify the ventilator setting at the bedside.
whenToUse: Use this on every mechanically ventilated patient with ARDS, suspected ARDS, or any acute hypoxemic respiratory failure where lung-protective ventilation is the standard of care - which, since the 2000 ARDSNet trial, is most of them. The trial showed an absolute mortality reduction with 6 mL/kg PBW versus 12 mL/kg PBW; using actual body weight in an overweight patient routinely produces injurious tidal volumes. The number is also worth checking on transport or after a vent swap. PBW does not account for amputation, severe kyphoscoliosis, or pregnancy; adjust clinically in those settings.
